reviews.title: Battery keeps fading/ very limited watch faces and limited app.
reviews.text: Dealing with the company was good, they offered to send another watch as I do like the look of it. However, after having the battery fade after 1.5 days and screen going black and not having many watch faces I decided not to bother with it anymore.  Maybe the new one would be fine but I had spent too much time on a simple procedure with watch. Also, the app is limited, it did not show how much battery there was left which seems strange. I returned it.

reviews.title: Great replacement bands for the Fitbit Charge 5
reviews.text: These bands fit well on the Fitbit Charge 5, as a replacement for a damaged band or just to change up the look now and then.  They are easy to install and remove and they do give an entirely different look to the Charge 5. I have the Large size and they are the same length as the original band that came with the Fitbit.  The colors as presented in listing are accurate and, in fact, I liked the teal and purple even more when I saw them in person.  There's only one noticeable difference between these bands and the original band.  The original band is quite smooth while these bands have some friction to them when you run you fingers over them.  Neither texture is better than the other. They're just different. My original band is black and absent the difference in feel, there's no real distinction vs. the black band that I received in this six pack.

reviews.title: Sooooo cute, soooo comfy
reviews.text: I am very picky when it comes to things touching my skin. My socks have to be on the correct foot, tags on clothes need to die, I’d rather wear my clothes inside out than feel a seam pressing into my skin. This band is exactly what I was looking for, barely there. It smells like real leather so I guess they weren’t lying about that. The color was labeled as blue but the picture looked greenish bluish grayish. In person it looks just like their picture and I’m happy about that. I’m petite and overweight (5’4 and 175lbs) and this size is perfect, plenty of room to go bigger or smaller. I adore the thinness of the band, no more bulky Fitbit!

reviews.title: So comfortable!
reviews.text: Best band I’ve ever worn! These are so comfortable and lightweight that I sometimes forgot I have my Fitbit on! It’s breathable! I like that it was so many holes that you can use to tighten with. Sometimes with others their so far apart that it’s righter too loose or too tight. Trust me, you will not have that issue with this band! The design is so beautiful and the colors are perfect!

reviews.title: Great bands
reviews.text: Comfortable and lots of holes to change size. Looks pretty and I like the thinner band.

reviews.title: Easier to set-up than previous bands
reviews.text:
I have really struggled getting replacement bands in place in the past. I had this one switched over in a matter of minutes. The lacy look makes it less apparent that the band is vinyl and the dark colors help to hide stains. I have bad metal allergies nd was a bit afraid of what would happen with the clasp given it is metal and has some contact with my skin. Been wearing this since December and the only time I have issues with a rash was when I had the watch fastened too tightly to my wrist. I believe this was from trapped sweat and moisture and not a metal allergy reaction.
Extra great deal in that you get three bands. I don't foresee a need to buy more bands for at least a year.

reviews.title: Not durable, hard to latch
reviews.text:
I liked the shape of this band and how it looked on my sense. Unfortunately it was less than a week before it started snagging on blankets and towels. Then it started snagging my tighter woven clothes. Within a couple of weeks the area that was snagging on things bent outward resulting in it scratching myself and others along with snagging anything that got close to it.

It is also very hard to do the clasp with one hand. At least if you have a reduced level of manual dexterity. I did figure out how to put it on. Taking it off is easier.

I can't recommend this item. I sent it back for a refund.

reviews.title: Colorful band
reviews.text: I love the color . The band narrows once it comes off the fitbit. My only dislike is the extra band goes under the band instead of on top.

reviews.title: I have a love hate relationship with this band.
reviews.text: I love the look of this band on my Fitbit, it matches perfectly. How you fasten it is kind of a pain. It slips through and runs under itself, which prevents a floppy tail of band, but it can be tricky to do and adjust. Everything else about the band is great. It's lightweight, matches my Fitbit perfectly, and is easy to clean.

reviews.title: Nice Band
reviews.text: I like having options when it comes to the band of my Fitbit Versa3. This one looks nice. It's a little less casual than the silicone ones. It fits my Versa fine and was easy to attach. I wear my Fitbit on my left arm (I'm right handed). I found it a little more difficult to put on my wrist than my other bands. I was able to get it, but it took a few tries. Once on my wrist, my Versa feels secure and I'm not concerned about it coming undone.

reviews.title: Super Cute
reviews.text: Loving this wristband. It fits great and stays on without problems. Totally adjustable and wears really nicely. Couldn't be happier with this wristband.

reviews.title: My daughter is obsessed with the watch
reviews.text: This is a perfect alternative of a smart watch. I didn't want to get a real smart watch for my daughter because they are so expensive but she kept insisting on it. Saw this kids watch the other day and bought. She thinks it's an apple watch Haha. She loved it and I'm so happy.

reviews.title: She Loves It!
reviews.text: Picture and video is clear and the games and other things on it are fun. This will also help children to learn time and definitely a good beginners watch for a kid. She figured out how to use many of the features without my help. Lots of great features. She likes to set her alarm clock every morning. This is a great first watch and a perfect gift.

reviews.title: It was ok.
reviews.text:
It's very cute. But let's talk about it.
The Camara was a bit blurry.
Never tried the music option so I don't know.
The pedometer would mark a step even when just moving hands so that wasn't accurate.
We had some trouble setting the alarm clock and set chores.
And the games were cute and fun but killed the battery fast.
I guess I was hopping too much out of this.
It was a gift for my daughter since her timex watch went out. But unfortunately it doesn't have a connect to phone feature where she could receive messages when I'm out and about running errands or late to pick her up from school for that matter. The battery didn't last long and even though my girl liked it, it wasn't too functional for what we needed it for. As I was going to return it my dog had other plans. So yeah the watch didn't survived it's attacker. So it's not dog proofed. Lol 😂

title: Samsung Galaxy Watch 3 (45mm, GPS, Bluetooth) Smart Watch with Advanced Health Monitoring, Fitness Tracking, and Long Lasting Battery - Mystic Black (US Version) (Renewed)
description: Spend your time moving while Galaxy Watch3 tracks seven popular activities automatically. Get more out of each movement thanks to a built-in run coaching.
features: Galaxy Watch3 combines powerful technology with a premium, customizable design so you can manage the day-today from your wrist, beautifully.
Keep an eye on wellness with advanced health monitoring, and go for days without charging.

Keep an eye on your fitness and health with SpO2, VO2 max, and heart-rate monitoring. Help is just a tap away if you stumble. And track your cycle in an easy and discreet way. *This device and related software are not intended for use in the diagnosis of disease or other conditions, or in the cure, mitigation, treatment or prevention of disease. SPO2, VO2 max and running coach features not available at the time of launch, will become available during Q3. Features will require a software update. **This device and related software are not intended for use in the diagnosis of disease or other conditions, or in the cure, mitigation, treatment or prevention of disease. ***Trip detection recognizes falls when engaged in dynamic motion, not when still. Requires network connection to notify emergency contact. GPS accuracy may be affected by obstructions to satellite signal such as buildings.
Galaxy Watch3 combines style—two sizes, three colors, 50,000-plus watch faces and premium leather bands—with military-grade durability and water resistance.
Spend your time moving while Galaxy Watch3 tracks seven popular activities automatically. Get more out of each movement thanks to a built-in run coaching.

reviews.title: Great watch, much better than my old "smart" watch
reviews.text:
I got Moto 360 several years ago when smart watches first came out and it was nice, but annoying. It was tied into everything and kept giving me alerts and so eventually I got tired of it. My wife also had a first generation Samsung Gear watch and that was about the same as the Moto.
Well, fast forward a few years and now my wife, daughter, son and daughter-in-law all have newer smart watches (Apple Watches and Fitbit) and they all love them. It was time to get a new phone, so I got a Samsung S20 FE and then decided to get the Samsung watch. I saw on the reviews that it really works well with Samsung phones and so I decided to get the Watch 3. I was looking at the Watch 2, which had a few less features and then I found the Watch 3 in the 41mm size and then found a refurbished one so I ended up paying about the same for the Watch 3 as I would have for the Watch 2.
I'm very impressed with the watch and really love it. I didn't get the one with a cell phone, it just connects to my phone with bluetooth and/or wifi, but it's really amazing. I get my messages, and can turn off the ones that I don't want and then I also get all the health apps. So, now it tells me to get off my chair and walk around and does EKG and stress tesing and Oxygen levels..
I was concerned that the 41 mm might be too small, but it is a great size for me as you can see from the pic. I have had a Citizen Echo Drive for more than 10 years and it is a little larger, but I like the size of the Watch 3 better.
If you are thinking about getting a watch, this one is a good deal right now and I believe it does work best if you use it with a Samsung phone.

reviews.title: Love the watch, hate the battery.
reviews.text: I don't use the sleep tracking because the watch is bulky and annoying while trying to sleep. The mobile app is ok, constantly running in background.  The battery life sucks, only good for a day and sometimes a day and a half which is to bad because I do love all the things it does. I think I can answer my phone on my watch (still figuring out how) can definitely hang up on unwanted calls and can read messages.  Good pedometer and workout tool. Overall nice watch with crappy battery life.

reviews.title: I like it.
reviews.text: I had to change out the band. The buckle was a pain. I must recharge every day.

reviews.title: Upgrade
reviews.text: I initially gave my mom the first samsung watch and when it went down, she let me have it. Because I initially spoiled her with the first one I figured it was time to upgrade her. And she loves it, her favorite accessory thus far....

reviews.title: The price! Bought refurbished and love it
reviews.text: Love the price, as I only paid $136 because it was refurbished, but looks brand new and works great! Two things that I'm disappointed with 1 no YouTube app to listen to free music 2 must buy a subscription to Spotify! To listen to music on the watch. Besides those two negative things. The watch is a great buy

title: Garmin Forerunner® 955 Solar, GPS Running Smartwatch with Solar Charging Capabilities, Tailored to Triathletes, Long-Lasting Battery, Whitestone
description: Lead the pack with Forerunner® 955 Solar, the premium GPS running and triathlon smartwatch with Power Glass™ solar charging to extend battery life. The training readiness feature tells you when you’re primed for a productive session — while daily suggested workouts adapt to your performance and recovery alongside the race widget, which provides training tips, course details and completion time predictions. With morning report, you can get an overview of your sleep, recovery, training and more — as well as a picture of your overall health with heart rate variability (HRV) status (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics). On the run, new multi-band GPS and full-color, built-in maps help you navigate confidently. And with traditional buttons and a touch display, it has never been easier to tap into your training status and know whether you’re training productively, peaking or strained. If you’re pushing toward the podium — or to the very limits — Forerunner 955 Solar is by your side with up to 20 days of battery life with solar charging in smartwatch mode (assuming all-day wear with 3 hours per day outside in 50,000 lux conditions).
features: Built with an always-on, full-color display that’s light on the wrist and easy to read even in direct sunlight

Battery life: up to 20 days of battery life in smartwatch mode for a full picture of your health — from sleep to training (assuming all-day wear with 3 hours per day outside in 50,000 lux conditions); up to 42 hours in GPS mode and up to 49 hours with solar charging (assuming continuous use for entire period in 50,000 lux conditions); up to 110 hours in UltraTrac mode with solar charging (assuming use in 50,000 lux conditions)
Click or swipe through maps and stats with traditional button controls or a new, responsive touchscreen
Whether you’re running on city streets or riding densely covered trails, Forerunner 955 provides full-color, built-in mapping to keep you on track

Access multiple global navigation satellite systems, and get access to multiple frequencies sent by navigation satellites for improved accuracy in areas where GNSS signals are reflected, weak or typically don't penetrate — giving you superior accuracy for how far and how fast you’ve run — even in challenging environments
From the moment you wake up, get a training readiness score based on your sleep quality, recovery, training and more, so you can determine whether it’s a good day to go hard — or take it easy
Get ready for your next race with race widget — which provides training tips, personalized daily suggested workouts and completion time predictions based on course details, weather and performance

Using new indicators such as HRV status, your recent exercise history and performance, get an easy insight into your overall effort with training status — and know whether you’re training productively, peaking or strained

Receive an overview of your sleep, recovery and training outlook as soon as you wake up with morning report — alongside HRV status, training readiness and weather; you can even customize your report to show what you want to see (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics)

With HRV status, gain a deeper understanding of your overall health, recovery and training performance through heart rate variability while you sleep, based on technology developed by our Firstbeat Analytics team (This device is intended to give an estimate of your activity and metrics)

reviews.title: A great watch for a runner
reviews.text: Haven't found anything I don't like about this watch.  Have begun following it's training advice, and it appears to be working.  Provides a wealth of interesting information.  One real plus for us old guys: the face is larger than the 735XT making it easier for these old eyes to read it.

reviews.title: Buena compra
reviews.text: Llego excelente, bien empacado

reviews.title: Resumen de datos y métricas de seguimiento
reviews.text: Verdaderamente útil, el seguimiento de sueño, entreno, potencia, y muchos mas datos que nos permiten dar un seguimiento más preciso del estado físico.

reviews.title: VO2 and race prediction are garbage
reviews.text:
For some reason with the 955 - Garmin decided to stop updating VO2 and race prediction - so if you have come to rely/look at that - don't get this watch. They have tried fixing it with the last few beta updates, but they can't seem to figure out how to correct it.

Multi-band GPS is nice - which honestly is the only reason we all haven't moved back to the 945/735 - which are AMAZING watches.

reviews.title: Fantastic update
reviews.text:
The 955 is a fantastic update over the previous 9XX, 7XX, and 6XX models. This update I'd much more communicative and provides loads more encouraging updates and workout suggestions. The interface is also more fluid than previous models. The return of touch to the user interface is nice but, optional.

Definitely worth going with
